zh.lifeSummary: 當Roger de Quincy 2nd Earl of Winchester於1195年出生在英國哈普爾維古姆時，他的父親Saer de Quincy 1st Earl of Winchester35歲，母親Margaret de Beaumont41歲。他於1217年與Helen de Galloway在英國英格蘭哈普爾維古姆結婚。他們至少是3個女兒的父母。他去世於1264年4月25日在英國諾薩普頓史爾布拉可利享年69歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭肯特布頓 蒙查爾希亞聖 彼得 亞德。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Quincy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places in France deriving their names from the Gallo-Roman personal name Quintus, meaning ‘fifth(-born)’ + the locative suffix -acum. The earliest bearer of the name in England, that of Saer de Quincy, ancestor of the earls of Winchester, was from Cuinchy in Pas-de-Calais, but the name may have been reinforced by immigrants from Quincy-sous-Sénard in Seine-et-Oise or Quincy-Voisins in Seine-et-Marne. A family of the name de Quincy held the title Earl of Winchester 1207–65. Descendants of this family later bore the name Quincey up to the time of the English essayist Thomas De Quincey (1785–1859), who changed his name from Quincey to De Quincey in 1796.

History: The American Quincy family were established in MA by Edmund Quincy in 1633. Fifth in descent was Josiah Quincy (1744–75), a leading patriot, who was sent to England to argue the colonists’ case in 1774. His son Josiah (1772–1864) was a powerful opponent of slavery, president of Harvard, and mayor of Boston, a post also held by several of his descendants. The traditional pronunciation is “Quinzy”.

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Roger de Quincy 2nd Earl of Winchester was born in 1195, in Winchester, Hampshire, England, his father, Saer de Quincy 1st Earl of Winchester, was 35 and his mother, Margaret de Beaumont, was 41. He married Helen de Galloway in 1217, in Winchester, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 daughters. He died on 25 April 1264, in Brackley, Northamptonshire, England, at the age of 69, and was buried in Saint Peter Churchyard, Boughton Monchelsea, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Helen de Galloway en 1217, à Winchester, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 filles. Il est décédé le 25 avril 1264, à Brackley, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterré à Saint Peter Churchyard, Boughton Monchelsea, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: Roger de Quincy 2nd Earl of Winchester(이)가 1195년 영국 함프실르 윈체스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Saer de Quincy 1st Earl of Winchester(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret de Beaumont(은)는 41세였다. 그는 1217년 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 윈체스터에서 Helen de Galloway(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1264년 4월 25일 영국 노르섬프토실르 브라클리에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 부그h턴 몬헬시 세인트 피터 처르c히아어드에 묻혔다.

ru.Saint Peter Churchyard, Brackley, Noirth: сеинт питир чурчярд, боугтон мончелсеа, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Roger de Quincy 2nd Earl of Winchester родился в 1195 г. в местности винчестир, хампшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Saer de Quincy 1st Earl of Winchester было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Beaumont, 41. Он женился на женщине по имени Helen de Galloway в 1217 г. в местности винчестир, хампшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 дочери. Он умер 25 апреля 1264 г. в местности браккли, нортамптоншир, Англия в возрасте 69 лет и был похоронен в местности сеинт питир чурчярд, боугтон мончелсеа, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания.
